title: Host loads a valid bundled plugin manifest at boot
preconditions: Roubo is built with the bundled github-com, ghe, and jira-self-hosted plugin directories under plugins/
No prior ~/.roubo/plugins/ entries
hostApiVersion is 1.0.0

expected: Each bundled plugin appears with status enabled or disabled
No errored or incompatible badges for the bundled set
Plugin id, version, source label 'Bundled', and one-line description are visible per card

title: Host marks a plugin with invalid manifest as errored and continues boot
preconditions: ~/.roubo/plugins/broken-plugin/roubo-plugin.yaml is missing the required 'entry' field

expected: broken-plugin appears with status errored
The error reason names the missing 'entry' field
Other plugins still load and are usable
The Roubo host did not exit

title: Host refuses to enable a plugin whose 'roubo' range excludes the current hostApiVersion
preconditions: A plugin declares roubo: ^2.0.0; current hostApiVersion is 1.0.0

expected: Status pill reads 'Incompatible'
Banner copy explains the host range mismatch
No child process is spawned for this plugin

title: Plugin runs in an isolated child Node process
preconditions: A bundled plugin is enabled

expected: A child Node process exists per enabled plugin instance
The child's stdin and stdout are piped to the host
The plugin's working directory matches its install path

title: Host and plugin exchange JSON-RPC framed messages over stdio
preconditions: A plugin is enabled

steps.action: Call a plugin method via the host RPC layer (e.g. listSourceCandidates)

expected: Each message has a Content-Length header and a JSON body
Requests and responses match by id
Plugin response satisfies the declared JSON-RPC shape

title: Manifest declares all four permission categories and host parses them
preconditions: A plugin manifest declares permissions.network.hosts, permissions.credentials.slots, permissions.filesystem.paths, and permissions.processes

expected: All four permission categories are present
Each has the same shape as the zod schema in shared/plugin-manifest-schema.ts

title: Third-party install shows the permissions dialog before enabling
preconditions: Roubo is running
User opens the Install plugin dialog

steps.action: Paste a valid Git URL pointing at a plugin repository
expected: Host clones the repo into ~/.roubo/plugins/<id>/
Dialog lists every declared permission category with detail
Dialog shows the Git URL as the install source
Install and enable button is the only path forward
Cancel reverts state with no partial install

title: host.fetch enforces the manifest network allowlist
preconditions: A plugin declares network.hosts: ['api.github.com']
Plugin is enabled

expected: host.fetch rejects with a structured permission error
No outbound network request is made
Denial is written to the plugin's log file

title: host.fetch surfaces raw response headers across the RPC boundary
preconditions: A plugin declares network.hosts: ['api.github.com']
Plugin is enabled

steps.action: Plugin calls host.fetch against an endpoint that returns ETag and Retry-After
expected: Plugin receives the ETag header value verbatim
Plugin receives the Retry-After header value verbatim
Plugin receives rate-limit headers (X-RateLimit-Remaining, X-RateLimit-Reset) verbatim

title: Self-signed TLS opt-in toggle is per-plugin and off by default
preconditions: A fresh plugin install with no prior config

expected: The 'Allow self-signed TLS certificates' Checkbox is unchecked
Help text explains the risk
A warning is shown explaining the risk
host.fetch for this plugin now uses a Dispatcher with rejectUnauthorized: false
Other plugins' Dispatchers are unaffected

title: Credential set / get round-trip via OS keyring on macOS
preconditions: Running on macOS
Keychain accessible

expected: Returned value equals 'secret-value'
No file under ~/.roubo/ contains the plaintext value
The credential is visible in Keychain Access under a Roubo-namespaced item

title: Credential set / get round-trip via secret-tool on Ubuntu desktop
preconditions: Running on Ubuntu with gnome-keyring or kwallet present
secret-tool CLI installed

expected: Returned value equals 'secret-value'
No file under ~/.roubo/ contains the plaintext value

title: Plugin can be disabled and re-enabled from the Plugins page
preconditions: An enabled plugin

steps.action: Click Disable on the plugin card
expected: Plugin child process exits within 5 seconds
Status pill changes to Disabled
Existing bench snapshots still render with the last-good badge

steps.action: Click Enable on the plugin card
expected: A fresh plugin child process is spawned
Status pill changes to Enabled

title: Per-call RPC timeout cancels a hanging plugin call
preconditions: A fixture plugin that hangs on listIssues

steps.action: Host calls listIssues with default timeout (30s)

expected: Host returns a structured timeout error to the caller
Plugin child process is not orphaned
Plugin remains running and can respond to subsequent calls

title: Plugin restart honours the 3-in-5-minutes budget
preconditions: A fixture plugin that exits immediately on every start

expected: Host attempts restart with exponential backoff
After the 3rd restart inside a 5-minute window, plugin status becomes errored
No further restart attempts occur until the user manually intervenes

title: Last-good issue snapshot is served while plugin is errored
preconditions: A plugin successfully fetched issues recently
Plugin then enters errored state

steps.action: Open the project that uses the plugin

expected: The last-good issues are visible
A global banner reads 'Showing the last successful issue snapshot from <Plugin Name>. The plugin is currently unavailable.'
Manage plugins link navigates to the Plugins page

title: Per-plugin log files are written and viewable in-app
preconditions: An enabled plugin that emits stdout and stderr output

steps.action: Cause the plugin to emit log output via host.logger.info / warn / error

expected: ~/.roubo/plugins/<id>/logs/current.log exists and contains the entries

steps.action: Open the View logs dialog from the Plugins page
expected: Recent entries are visible without leaving Roubo
Entries include timestamp, level, and message

title: Plugins page exposes install, configure, enable, disable, view logs, uninstall actions
preconditions: The Plugins page is open with at least one bundled and one third-party plugin

expected: Configure, View logs, Enable/Disable are present on all cards
Uninstall is present only on third-party cards
Install plugin button is present in the page header

title: Local-directory third-party install validates the manifest before enabling
preconditions: A local directory at /tmp/my-plugin with a valid roubo-plugin.yaml

steps.action: Open Install plugin, switch to 'From local directory' tab, enter /tmp/my-plugin

expected: Permissions dialog shows the local path as the source
On accept, plugin appears on the Plugins page

title: A project can only have one active integration
preconditions: A project with the github-com plugin selected

steps.action: Open the Issue source tile and click Switch integration

expected: Effective config now has integration.plugin = jira-self-hosted
github-com is no longer the project's active source
Confirmation dialog explained the bench-survival behaviour before the switch

title: Source picker renders multi-list shape for GitHub.com / GHE
preconditions: Active integration is github-com or ghe

expected: A single combined list shows repositories and Projects with type labels
Selection across types is supported
Selected items appear in a removable chip strip

title: Source picker renders categorized-multi-list shape for Jira
preconditions: Active integration is jira-self-hosted

expected: Three tabs are present: Boards, Epics, Filters
Each tab has its own search field and list
Tab badges show per-category selected counts

title: Cross-source dedup keys on (integrationId, externalId)
preconditions: A GitHub issue is reachable via both a selected Project and a selected repo

expected: The issue appears exactly once
Its key matches (github-com, '<repo>#<number>')

title: Normalized issue contract carries the expected fields
preconditions: A plugin returns an issue from listIssues

steps.action: Inspect the issue object received by the host
expected: Fields present: integrationId, externalId, externalUrl, title, body, currentState, allowedTransitions, assignees, labels, issueType, blocks, blockedBy, updatedAt, raw
No deprecated fields (sprint, fixVersion, custom fields, attachments, comments, parent, children, epic) appear

title: listIssues is paginated with default page size 50
preconditions: A plugin configured with default page size
Source contains at least 120 issues

expected: First page returns up to 50 items and a nextCursor
Subsequent pages are fetched via useInfiniteQuery
UI does not block on any individual page

title: roubo.yaml + user override deep-merge with per-field optionality
preconditions: roubo.yaml: integration: { plugin: jira-self-hosted, instance: 'https://jira.acme.com' }
User override: integration: { sources: { boards: [12] } }

expected: plugin = jira-self-hosted (from roubo.yaml)
instance = 'https://jira.acme.com' (from roubo.yaml)
sources.boards = [12] (from override)

title: Array merge semantics REPLACE, not concat
preconditions: roubo.yaml: integration.sources.boards = [12, 34]
User override: integration.sources.boards = [99]

expected: sources.boards equals [99]
Values 12 and 34 are NOT present in the effective config

title: Missing-plugin prompt on project load
preconditions: roubo.yaml references plugin: 'jira-self-hosted'
jira-self-hosted is NOT installed locally

expected: Dialog reads 'Plugin needed for this project'
If roubo.yaml carries integration.pluginSource, a one-click Install button is offered using that source
If not, the user can paste a Git URL or local path
Plugin installs
Project loads normally

title: Jira plugin maps default 'blocks' and 'is blocked by' link types
preconditions: A Jira instance with default link type names
Issue PROJ-200 is linked 'blocks' to PROJ-201

steps.action: Plugin returns the normalized PROJ-200 issue

expected: PROJ-200.blocks contains 'PROJ-201'
PROJ-201.blockedBy contains 'PROJ-200'
Other Jira link types are ignored

title: Jira plugin polls incrementally via JQL updated >=
preconditions: Plugin has a stored last-poll timestamp for a source

expected: JQL includes 'updated >= <last-poll>'
Plugin updates its last-poll timestamp on successful response

title: First-launch migration of an existing GitHub.com project
preconditions: A pre-plugin ~/.roubo with auth.json (plaintext token), projects.json referencing a GitHub.com Project, and state.json with assignedIssue numeric fields

expected: bundled github-com plugin is enabled
OAuth token moved to OS keyring slot via host.credentials.set
Per-user override file ~/.roubo/integrations/<projectId>.yaml is created with the existing GitHub Project as a selected source
state.json schemaVersion bumped
auth.json is deleted only AFTER schemaVersion bump
Re-running the binary is a no-op
One-time migration banner shown

title: Bench-issue snapshot carries integrationId and externalId
preconditions: A migrated bench from a pre-plugin Roubo

expected: integrationId is 'github-com'
externalId is the string form of the legacy number

steps.action: Create a new bench against a new integration
expected: integrationId reflects the new active integration

title: Issue type mappings endpoint sources strings from the active plugin
preconditions: A project with jira-self-hosted active; the Jira plugin exposes issue types ['Bug', 'Story', 'Epic']

expected: Response lists the strings from the Jira plugin
Shape Record<string, string> is preserved on the mappings PUT

title: Soft block: bench creation proceeds with a banner when issue has open blockers
preconditions: Issue PROJ-200 has blockedBy: ['PROJ-100']
PROJ-100 is open

steps.action: Open the Issue Picker on PROJ-200

expected: Amber-tinted banner names PROJ-100 as an open blocker
Create bench button remains enabled
Bench is created successfully
No hard-block error is thrown

title: Plugin SDK exports definePlugin and host helpers
preconditions: @roubo/plugin-sdk is published or available as a local package

steps.action: Author imports definePlugin and calls it with a contract object

expected: RPC binding is established without the author writing stdio framing
host.fetch, host.credentials, host.logger are reachable inside the contract methods

title: Manifest JSON Schema artifact published at schema/roubo-plugin.schema.json
preconditions: A built Roubo distribution

expected: File exists
Shape matches the runtime zod schema in shared/plugin-manifest-schema.ts
An invalid roubo-plugin.yaml fails validation against it in an IDE-class JSON Schema validator

title: Test connection button calls plugin.validateConfig and reports inline
preconditions: GHE plugin configure dialog is open with instance URL and PAT entered

expected: validateConfig is called with the entered config
On success: result strip shows green check and 'Connected as <Display Name>'
On auth failure: red dot and a structured error message
Save button is enabled only after a successful test for the current values

title: Identity capture: getCurrentUser is called once and persisted
preconditions: Test connection has succeeded with a valid PAT

expected: The captured external user id is persisted on the project's integration record
Subsequent assign operations use this id as the 'me' target

title: Transition dropdown lists only allowedTransitions
preconditions: A bench against an issue whose allowedTransitions is ['In Review', 'Blocked']

expected: Exactly two entries: 'In Review' and 'Blocked'
No other states (e.g. 'Done', 'To Do') are shown

title: Assign-to-me uses the captured identity
preconditions: The user's captured identity is 'jane.doe@acme.com'
A bench against an issue with assignees: []

expected: plugin.assignIssue is called with externalId and 'jane.doe@acme.com'
On success, assignees array updates to include jane.doe@acme.com optimistically

title: Forward-compat paper sketch exists and is referenced from architecture.md
preconditions: The slug folder contains the paper sketch

steps.action: Verify the sketch exists under .specifications/integration-plugins/ and is referenced from architecture.md

expected: Sketch outlines AI-agent plugin manifest fields and methods
Sketch outlines project-component plugin manifest fields and methods
Sketch passes through the same host RPC primitives without proposed major-version changes

title: GitHub.com plugin preserves ETag short-circuiting
preconditions: The bundled github-com plugin is enabled
A prior request stored an ETag for /repos/foo/bar/issues

steps.action: Plugin issues a second request to the same URL

expected: Plugin sends If-None-Match with the stored ETag
On 304 response, plugin short-circuits and returns the cached payload
ETag store survives across plugin process restarts only inasmuch as the plugin persists it; otherwise behaviour matches the live source

title: GitHub.com plugin honours Retry-After on secondary rate-limit
preconditions: The bundled github-com plugin is enabled
A request elicits a 429 with Retry-After: 30

steps.action: Plugin issues a subsequent request to the same endpoint

expected: Plugin waits at least 30 seconds before retrying
Plugin surfaces a structured rate-limit error to the host if the budget is exceeded

title: Permission denial is logged with stable identifiers
preconditions: A plugin attempts an out-of-allowlist host.fetch

expected: A line containing pluginId, methodName, and the denied URL is present
Banner copy on the Plugins page references the same pluginId.methodName identifier

title: Roubo never persists 'raw' beyond an active bench's assignedIssue
preconditions: A plugin returns a normalized issue with a populated 'raw' field

steps.action: Assign the issue to a bench

expected: The active bench's assignedIssue.raw is present
No 'raw' field remains anywhere in state.json after the bench is cleared

title: Plugins UI keyboard navigation and focus management
preconditions: Plugins page is open

steps.action: Press Tab repeatedly through the page
expected: Focus moves through Install plugin button, each plugin card, and each card action in a sensible order
Visible focus ring is present on every focusable element

steps.action: Open the Install dialog and press Escape
expected: Dialog closes
Focus returns to the Install plugin button

title: End-to-end: GHE developer connects their corporate instance
preconditions: GHE plugin is bundled
User has a valid GHE PAT and instance URL

steps.action: Open Plugins page, enable GHE plugin
Open project Issue source tile, choose GHE
Enter instance URL and PAT in the configure dialog

expected: Returns 'Connected as <user>' inline
multi-list picker shows the user's repos and Projects

steps.action: Select at least one repo and one Project, click Select

expected: Issues from the selected sources appear
Cross-source dedup applies

title: End-to-end: Jira developer connects their self-hosted instance
preconditions: Jira plugin is bundled
User has a valid Jira Data Center PAT and instance URL

steps.action: Configure plugin: enter instance URL and PAT

expected: Returns 'Connected as <displayName>' inline

steps.action: Open Configure sources, select a board, an epic, and a filter

expected: Issues appear; blocks/blocked-by are honoured (soft banner where applicable)
Issue type strings from Jira drive the issue-type mappings

title: End-to-end: existing GitHub.com user is migrated invisibly
preconditions: A real pre-plugin ~/.roubo fixture is set up (auth.json, projects.json with GitHub Project, state.json with two assigned benches)

expected: Single one-time success banner
Issue sources are pre-selected from the GitHub Project
Existing assigned benches still link to their issues
auth.json deleted
state.json schemaVersion bumped
~/.roubo/integrations/<projectId>.yaml created per migrated project
OS keyring contains the OAuth token

title: End-to-end: power user writes and installs a local plugin
preconditions: @roubo/plugin-sdk is available; user has a local /tmp/linear-plugin/ directory with a valid roubo-plugin.yaml and entry point

steps.action: Open Install plugin, From local directory, enter /tmp/linear-plugin
Configure plugin with Linear API token
Add the plugin as the project's integration

expected: Linear issues appear in the normalized shape

title: End-to-end: teammate clones a repo with a plugin not installed
preconditions: A repo's roubo.yaml declares plugin: jira-self-hosted with pluginSource hint
Teammate's Roubo has jira-self-hosted NOT installed

expected: One-click Install button is shown using the pluginSource

steps.action: Configure the plugin (teammate enters their own credentials)

expected: Teammate sees their own slice of issues per the merged effective config

title: End-to-end: project owner switches integration with active benches
preconditions: A project on github-com with two active benches against GitHub issues

steps.action: Open Switch integration, pick jira-self-hosted, confirm

expected: Each bench shows the 'Issue from previous integration' badge
Source-sync controls are visibly disabled for those benches
New bench uses jira-self-hosted as the integration
Issue source tile shows Jira sources

title: End-to-end: install third-party plugin from Git URL
preconditions: A reachable Git URL hosting a valid plugin

steps.action: Open Install plugin, From Git URL tab, paste the URL

expected: Host clones into ~/.roubo/plugins/<id>/

steps.action: Confirm plugin is enabled and reachable from a project's Issue source tile

title: End-to-end: user transitions an issue from Roubo
preconditions: A bench against an issue whose allowedTransitions = ['In Review', 'Done']

expected: plugin.applyTransition is called
Optimistic UI updates the state pill to 'In Review'
On success, the source system reflects the transition on next refresh

title: End-to-end: plugin failure visible with last-good snapshot and recovery
preconditions: A plugin successfully fetched issues; plugin then crashes 3 times within 5 minutes

expected: Plugin status is errored after the 3rd crash

steps.action: Open a project that uses this plugin
expected: Last-good snapshot is served with a global banner

steps.action: Click Restart on the errored card
expected: Restart counter clears
Plugin starts; status becomes Enabled

title: End-to-end: per-user override coexists with empty roubo.yaml
preconditions: Project's roubo.yaml has no integration block
User creates a per-user override picking jira-self-hosted with one filter

expected: plugin = jira-self-hosted
instance and sources from override only
Issue source tile caption reads 'Configuration from your override; roubo.yaml has no integration block'
No changes to roubo.yaml (override is per-user, not committed)

title: End-to-end: full paginated scan of 500 issues
preconditions: A source with exactly 500 issues
Page size = 50

steps.action: Open the issue picker and scroll to the bottom

expected: 10 pages are fetched in sequence via useInfiniteQuery
Each page's spinner-to-rendered round trip is bounded; the UI does not block scroll
Final count of rendered issues is 500

title: Edge: install dialog rejects an unreachable Git URL gracefully
preconditions: Install dialog open

expected: Inline red banner reports 'Could not clone repository. git exited with code 128: Repository not found.'
No partial directory left in ~/.roubo/plugins/
User can correct the URL and try again

title: Edge: install dialog rejects a directory without roubo-plugin.yaml
preconditions: Install dialog open

steps.action: Paste a local directory path that has no roubo-plugin.yaml

expected: Inline red banner names the missing manifest
No plugin appears on the Plugins page

title: Edge: validateConfig returns auth failure on bad PAT
preconditions: GHE configure dialog open with a valid instance URL but a junk PAT

expected: validateConfig returns auth-failed error
Result strip shows red dot and the structured error: 'Authentication failed: 401 Unauthorized. Check that the token is valid and has the required scopes.'
Save remains disabled

title: Edge: validateConfig returns network error when instance is unreachable
preconditions: Jira configure dialog with a non-resolving instance URL

expected: Result strip shows red dot and 'Could not reach <URL>: ENOTFOUND. Check the URL and your VPN.'
Save remains disabled

title: Edge: validateConfig surfaces TLS error and offers inline opt-in
preconditions: Jira instance is reachable but uses a self-signed cert
Self-signed TLS is off

expected: Result strip shows red dot and 'TLS error: self-signed certificate.'
Inline Enable button is offered
Self-signed TLS toggle flips on with the warning shown
Test connection re-runs and now succeeds

title: Edge: applyTransition fails with permission error
preconditions: A bench against an issue with allowedTransitions = ['Done']
User's token lacks transition permission on this workflow

expected: plugin.applyTransition is called
Plugin returns a structured error 'Your token lacks permission to transition this workflow.'
UI reverts the optimistic update
Inline red banner under the Transition control surfaces the error verbatim

title: Edge: applyTransition is not auto-fired on bench lifecycle events
preconditions: A bench is created against an issue in state 'To Do'

expected: No applyTransition call was sent for any of the lifecycle events
Source state remains 'To Do' unless the user explicitly transitioned

title: Edge: deep-merge with array REPLACE behaviour, override sets empty array
preconditions: roubo.yaml: integration.sources.boards = [12]
User override: integration.sources.boards = []

expected: Effective sources.boards equals []
Empty array in override is a valid replacement, not 'unset'

title: Edge: pluginSource hint in roubo.yaml points at a Git URL no longer reachable
preconditions: roubo.yaml carries integration.pluginSource pointing at a removed repository
Teammate does not have the plugin installed

expected: One-click Install button attempts the pluginSource URL
Clone fails with structured error
User is offered to paste a different Git URL or local directory path

title: Edge: schema-strict roubo.yaml rejects unknown integration fields
preconditions: roubo.yaml: integration: { plugin: 'jira-self-hosted', bogus_field: true }

expected: zod schema rejects 'bogus_field' with a clear error
Roubo refuses to load the project until the file is fixed
Existing files without an integration block continue to validate

title: Edge: migration is a no-op when ~/.roubo is already on the post-plugin schemaVersion
preconditions: Migration has already completed once; schemaVersion is bumped

expected: Migration code path detects the bumped schemaVersion and exits early
No files are modified
No migration banner is shown a second time

title: Edge: migration mid-flight failure rolls back
preconditions: Migration is run with the OS keyring temporarily unavailable

steps.action: Migration attempts to move auth.json token into keyring; keyring set fails

expected: auth.json is still present (NOT deleted)
schemaVersion is unchanged (NOT bumped)
No partial integration override file remains
Roubo surfaces the rolled-back-migration banner with a link to the Plugins page

title: Edge: plugin attempts host.credentials.get on a slot it did not declare
preconditions: Plugin manifest declares credentials.slots: ['jira-token']
Plugin attempts host.credentials.get('github-token')

expected: Host returns a structured permission error
Plugin does NOT receive the credential value
Denial is logged with stable pluginId.methodName identifier

title: Edge: pagination from a plugin that ignores the cursor
preconditions: A misbehaving plugin that always returns the same page regardless of cursor

steps.action: Open the issue picker and scroll

expected: Host detects an unchanging nextCursor or duplicate items by (integrationId, externalId) and stops fetching
UI shows a 'Plugin paging appears stuck. Try a refresh.' inline note
Roubo does not crash or loop

title: Edge: Jira plugin honours configured link-type rename
preconditions: A Jira instance where 'blocks' is renamed to 'depends on'
Plugin config sets blocksLinkTypeName = 'depends on'

expected: The custom-named link is correctly mapped to blocks / blockedBy

title: Edge: plugin sends a malformed JSON-RPC reply
preconditions: A fixture plugin that emits a chunk of non-JSON to stdout

expected: Host surfaces a parse error
Outstanding RPC promises reject with a structured error
Plugin remains running (single bad message does not crash plugin or host)

title: Edge: plugin sends an oversized JSON-RPC reply (10MB+)
preconditions: A fixture plugin that returns a 10MB+ payload for listIssues

expected: Host either handles the message or rejects with a size-limit error
Host does not crash or stall the other plugins
Behaviour is documented in the plugin SDK guide

title: Accessibility: install permissions dialog has clear screen-reader semantics
preconditions: A screen reader (VoiceOver on macOS or Orca on Linux) is running

expected: Dialog announces its title and role
Each permission category is announced with its label and detail
The install button is clearly identified as the primary action
Cancel button is clearly identified

title: Accessibility: source picker focus and keyboard nav
preconditions: categorized-multi-list source picker is open

expected: Focus moves predictably from tab list to items to selected-strip
Arrow keys navigate items inside a tab
Selection state updates and is announced

title: Reliability: host shutdown tears down plugin children
preconditions: Two plugins are running

expected: Plugin children receive SIGTERM and exit within 5 seconds
No plugin processes outlive the host
Logs record clean shutdown

title: Reliability: orphan plugin child after host hard crash receives SIGHUP on parent death
preconditions: Host is killed with SIGKILL
Plugin children are spawned with detached: false (the default)

expected: Child processes receive SIGHUP on parent exit on POSIX systems
Children exit shortly after; no zombies

title: Reliability: paginated fetch survives a single page error
preconditions: Plugin returns successfully for page 1, errors for page 2, returns successfully for page 3

expected: Page 1 renders
Page 2 surfaces a per-page error banner with retry
Page 3 renders without re-fetching page 1 or 2

title: Security: plugin attempts file write outside its plugin dir
preconditions: Plugin manifest declares no filesystem permission beyond its plugin dir

expected: Host-mediated filesystem operation is denied

If the plugin bypasses host helpers and uses raw fs (which is possible in Node), this is documented as a limitation, and Spike B includes a test that the host's audit log records the bypass via process-monitoring

title: Exploratory: large Jira instance with hundreds of filters in the picker
preconditions: A Jira instance with 300+ filters visible to the user

steps.action: Open the source picker, Filters tab

expected: List renders without freezing the UI
Search filtering responds within 200ms
If unacceptable, file a follow-on to paginate listSourceCandidates

title: Exploratory: third-party plugin with unusual config schema
preconditions: A community plugin whose configSchema includes nested objects and arrays

expected: Form renders without breaking the layout
Validation messages appear inline
Any pathological schema is documented as needing renderer improvement in a follow-on

title: Exploratory: plugin runs on Ubuntu headless (Spike A target)
preconditions: A clean headless Ubuntu environment (no graphical session)
secret-tool present, gnome-keyring-daemon not running by default

steps.action: Run the documented dbus-run-session + gnome-keyring-daemon --start --components=secrets recipe
Set and get a credential via host.credentials
expected: Credentials work after the recipe is applied
If the recipe fails, the open Spike A unknown is updated with the failing condition

title: Exploratory: forward-compat paper sketch is followed when scoping AI-agent plugin slug
preconditions: Slug for AI-agent plugins is in interview / feasibility

steps.action: Compare the AI-agent slug's manifest and method-set proposal to the paper sketch

expected: No host-API major-version bump is required
Any deviations from the sketch are recorded as decisions with rationale
The paper sketch's predictions are validated or revised in real time

title: Configure dialog renders three independent security & quality checkboxes per github.com source
preconditions: github.com plugin is enabled and configured with at least one repo source
Configure dialog is open for the github.com plugin
The 2026-05-24 addendum is built

steps.action: Expand the 'Security & quality alerts' disclosure for the configured source

expected: Three React Aria Checkbox rows: Code Scanning alerts, Secret Scanning alerts, Dependabot alerts
All three default unchecked
Help text under each names the category and any GitHub Advanced Security or admin requirement
Toggling any single checkbox does not toggle the other two

title: Enabling all three categories on a healthy github.com source returns alerts merged with regular Issues
preconditions: Source is a public github.com repo with at least one open Issue, one open Code Scanning alert, one open Secret Scanning alert, and one open Dependabot alert
Stored token has security_events scope and the user is a repo admin
All three category checkboxes are enabled for the source

steps.action: Trigger a listIssues pull from the Issues panel

expected: Regular Issue appears with no category chip
Code Scanning alert appears with chip 'CodeQL'
Secret Scanning alert appears with chip 'Secret'
Dependabot alert appears with chip 'Dependabot'
Each alert's external id has the form '<owner>/<repo>#<category>-<n>'

title: Both bundled plugins import alert fetchers from the shared workspace package
preconditions: plugins/_shared-github/ workspace package exists
plugins/github-com/ and plugins/ghe/ each declare a workspace dependency on it

steps.action: Run a workspace dependency audit (npm ls --workspaces)

expected: Both plugins resolve `@roubo/shared-github` (or the chosen package name) to plugins/_shared-github/
Neither plugin declares its own copy of the alert fetcher functions
@roubo/shared-github is NOT exported from plugin-sdk/ (decision per decisions-log.md 2026-05-24)

title: Secret Scanning raw payload is redacted before being placed in the normalized issue
preconditions: Test fixture: a Secret Scanning alert REST response that includes a known secret value 'ghp_AAAA1111BBBB2222CCCC3333'
Secret Scanning is enabled on the source

steps.action: Invoke the alert-to-normalized mapper against the fixture

expected: raw.secret_prefix equals 'ghp_***' or first 4 chars + redaction marker
raw does NOT contain the full secret string 'ghp_AAAA1111BBBB2222CCCC3333' anywhere
Plugin stdout and stderr captured during the test contain neither the full secret nor raw

title: Code Scanning raw payload strips embedded code snippets
preconditions: Test fixture: a Code Scanning alert REST response with `most_recent_instance.location.snippet` populated
Code Scanning is enabled on the source

steps.action: Invoke the alert-to-normalized mapper against the fixture

expected: raw.location includes file path and line/column
raw does NOT include the snippet text
Plugin stdout and stderr contain neither the snippet nor raw

title: External-id format prevents collision between Issue #17 and Code Scanning alert #17 on the same repo
preconditions: Fixture: repo wday-planning/roubo has an Issue numbered 17 AND a Code Scanning alert numbered 17
Code Scanning is enabled for the source

expected: Issue 17 has externalId 'wday-planning/roubo#17'
Code Scanning alert 17 has externalId 'wday-planning/roubo#code-scanning-17'
Dedup logic does not collapse the two entries
Bench creation on each succeeds without raising 'duplicate external id'

title: Enabling a category on a source with a scope-lacking token surfaces inline re-consent action
preconditions: Stored OAuth token's scope set does NOT include security_events
OAuth app's scope set now includes security_events
Configure dialog is open for a github.com source

steps.action: Toggle Dependabot alerts ON for the source

expected: A warning chip appears with cause 'token lacks security_events permission. Click to upgrade.'
Save is blocked until the chip's action is taken
Clicking the chip opens the OAuth re-consent dialog
Completing the OAuth flow replaces the stored token in place and clears the chip on the next probe

title: User who never enables a category never sees an OAuth re-consent prompt after scope-set upgrade
preconditions: Stored OAuth token predates the 2026-05-24 scope-set upgrade
User has no source with any security category enabled

steps.action: Launch Roubo and load any project with a github.com integration
Observe top-of-shell, project view, Plugins page, and Configure dialog
expected: No re-consent prompt appears anywhere
Regular Issues continue to fetch and render normally
No banner mentions security alerts or new scopes

title: Code Scanning disabled on the repo: category gracefully skipped with warning chip
preconditions: Source is a public repo with Code Scanning explicitly disabled (HTTP 404 from /code-scanning/alerts)
Token has security_events scope
Code Scanning checkbox is enabled for the source

expected: Pull succeeds; regular Issues and any other enabled categories return normally
Source row in Configure dialog shows a warning chip 'Code Scanning unavailable: GHAS not enabled on this repo.' (or equivalent HTTP-404 cause copy)
No error toast or hard failure surfaced to the user

title: Test connection probes each enabled category and renders per-category status
preconditions: Configure dialog open for a source with all three categories enabled
Token has security_events; Secret Scanning is unavailable because the repo is a private repo without GHAS

expected: Row 'Connected as @<user>.'
Row 'Issues: ok (read 1 item).'
Row 'Code Scanning: ok (read 1 item).'
Row 'Secret Scanning: requires GitHub Advanced Security on private repos.'
Row 'Dependabot: ok (read 1 item).'
Overall result is success; the unavailable Secret Scanning row uses an amber-warning icon but does NOT fail the overall test

title: Alert-backed bench hides Transition dropdown and disables Assign control
preconditions: A bench is created from a Code Scanning alert
The bench's assignedIssue.issueType is 'security-code-scanning'

expected: No Transition dropdown is rendered
In its place is the muted line 'Resolved by pushing code that fixes the underlying alert. GitHub auto-closes the alert.'
Assign control is rendered but disabled
Tooltip on the disabled Assign reads 'Security alerts cannot be assigned from Roubo. They are repo-level findings, not user-assigned work.'
Start / stop / clear / logs / terminal all function normally

title: Blueprint-by-issue-type resolver accepts the three new security issue type strings
preconditions: Existing blueprint-by-issue-type UI is open for a project linked to a github.com source
At least one blueprint exists in the project

steps.action: Open the issue-type dropdown in the mapping UI

expected: 'security-code-scanning' appears as a selectable type
'security-secret-scanning' appears as a selectable type
'security-dependabot' appears as a selectable type
Mapping any of the three to a blueprint persists and a subsequent bench created from an alert of that category uses the mapped blueprint

title: Toggling a category OFF does not mutate or clear existing alert-backed benches
preconditions: Two benches exist whose assignedIssue.issueType is 'security-dependabot' from the same source
Dependabot alerts checkbox is currently ON for that source

steps.action: Toggle Dependabot alerts OFF for the source and Save
Trigger a listIssues pull and observe the benches
expected: Both existing benches remain present and operational
Each bench's assignedIssue title, externalId, externalUrl, and category chip remain unchanged
The Configure dialog source row shows muted help text '2 existing benches still show alerts from this category.'
No new Dependabot alerts appear in the next listIssues pull

title: p95 listIssues round-trip stays under 8 seconds for 5 sources x 200 items warm with ETag/304
preconditions: 5 github.com sources configured, each with ~200 open items split across regular Issues + the three security categories
All three categories enabled on every source
ETag store is warm from a prior pull (so 304 short-circuit is in play)

steps.action: Trigger 30 sequential warm listIssues pulls and record wall-clock latency per pull

expected: p95 latency is under 8000ms
Issues-only baseline (run separately with all three categories disabled) is recorded; p95 of that baseline plus 10% is the regression gate
All-categories-disabled p95 is not more than baseline * 1.10

title: Warning chip and inline re-consent action are accessible per WCAG 2.1 AA
preconditions: Configure dialog open with a source whose Dependabot alerts checkbox is enabled and stored token lacks security_events

steps.action: Navigate to the warning chip via keyboard only (Tab)
Verify keyboard reachability and screen-reader announcements
expected: Tab focus lands on the warning chip with a visible focus ring
Enter or Space activates the chip and opens the re-consent dialog
Screen reader announces the chip's accessible name (cause text)
State change from idle to 'Connecting...' to 'Waiting for browser...' to clear is announced via React Aria live region
axe-core scan of the dialog reports zero serious violations

title: Fine-grained GHE PAT without X-OAuth-Scopes header surfaces graceful warning copy
preconditions: GHE plugin configured with a fine-grained PAT (no X-OAuth-Scopes header on responses)
User enables Dependabot alerts for the source

steps.action: Observe Configure dialog warning chip for the source
expected: Warning chip cause reads 'Unable to verify token scopes. If category data is missing, regenerate your token with the security alert permission.' (or the documented graceful copy)
Chip does NOT assert 'token lacks security_events' as a definite truth
Plugin still attempted the Dependabot probe and the resulting HTTP code (if non-2xx) is captured in observability counters

title: Secret Scanning HTTP 451 (GHAS not enabled on private repo) renders the GHAS-specific cause string
preconditions: Source is a private repo without GitHub Advanced Security
Secret Scanning checkbox is enabled
Token has security_events scope

expected: Warning chip cause is 'Secret Scanning unavailable: requires GitHub Advanced Security on private repos.'
Other categories' fetch state is unaffected
Subsequent successful pull of Secret Scanning (after GHAS is enabled) clears the chip automatically

title: Dependabot HTTP 403 (caller is not repo admin) renders the admin-specific cause string
preconditions: Source is a repo where the user is not a repo admin
Dependabot checkbox is enabled
Token has security_events scope

expected: Warning chip cause distinguishes 'not a repo admin' from 'missing scope'
Other categories continue to fetch unaffected
Plugin observability counters record the 403 against the Dependabot endpoint, not the generic catch-all bucket

title: Test connection per-category probe times out without failing the overall test
preconditions: Code Scanning endpoint for the test source is artificially slow (exceeds per-probe 5s cap but other probes return quickly)
All three categories enabled

steps.action: Click Test connection and wait for the result

expected: Issues row: ok
Code Scanning row: 'Timed out'
Secret Scanning and Dependabot rows: ok
Overall result is success (validateConfig still returned successfully and the Connected-as line is present)
Total wall-clock for the strip is within the 12s overall budget

title: Rate-limit (HTTP 429) on a Dependabot endpoint backs off without poisoning other categories
preconditions: GitHub.com plugin sees an HTTP 429 with Retry-After: 30 on /dependabot/alerts for one of the sources
All three categories enabled

steps.action: Trigger a listIssues pull during the 429

expected: Dependabot fetch is skipped for this pull with a warning chip cause naming the rate-limit reset
Code Scanning and Secret Scanning fetches complete normally
Next pull initiated after Retry-After respects the reset window before re-attempting Dependabot
Plugin error path receives a structured rate-limit error with the reset timestamp

title: End-to-end OAuth re-consent flow upgrades the token and the next pull returns Dependabot alerts
preconditions: Stored OAuth token lacks security_events
OAuth app's scope set includes security_events
User opens Configure dialog and toggles Dependabot ON for a source

steps.action: Click the inline 'Click to upgrade.' chip action
Click 'Continue to GitHub' on the re-consent dialog
Complete the OAuth authorize flow in the system browser
Wait for the roubo://oauth/github/callback deep link
Observe the chip and trigger a listIssues pull
expected: Configure dialog stayed open through the round-trip
Stored token in the keyring slot is replaced in place (old token is no longer valid)
Token-scope cache is invalidated for that token id
Chip clears on the next probe; if a different category cause now applies (e.g. not-a-repo-admin), the new cause renders
Next listIssues pull returns Dependabot alerts merged with regular Issues

title: End-to-end bench creation from an alert uses the security-category blueprint mapping
preconditions: A blueprint mapping exists: 'security-dependabot' -> 'dep-update-blueprint'
A Dependabot alert appears in the merged Issues list for a github.com source

steps.action: Right-click the alert in the Issues list and select 'Create bench'

expected: Bench is created with the dep-update-blueprint injected into the workspace
assignedIssue.issueType is 'security-dependabot'
assignedIssue.externalId follows the namespaced format
blocks and blockedBy are empty
Transition is hidden and Assign is disabled per FR-048

title: Scale: 5 sources x 3 categories warm pull stays within latency budget with ETag/304 short-circuit
preconditions: 5 sources configured with all categories on; each source's previous pull populated the ETag store
Test harness records per-endpoint If-None-Match request count and 304 response count

steps.action: Run 100 warm pulls back-to-back and capture latency + cache-hit ratio

expected: p95 latency stays under 8s (NFR-013)
304 short-circuit hit rate for unchanged endpoints is >= 90% across the 100 runs
No category endpoint exceeds the rate-limit budget across the run
